title: papaver rhoeas var. trifidum
intro: Papaver rhoeas var. trifidum, also known as the Shirley poppy, is a variety of the common poppy plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's medicinal properties come from its alkaloid content, particularly thebaine, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including pain, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas var. trifidum include its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although caution is advised due to the potential for side effects and interactions with other medications.
